scenario_name: Public-sector citizen-services chatbot over published agency content
description: A municipal or national government agency deploys a citizen-services chatbot to handle high-volume inquiries about forms, benefits eligibility, and process steps. The chatbot retrieves over a published, publicly-available agency content corpus. Authentication is optional; authenticated sessions may receive personalized status updates from a limited read-only tool. The agency operates under public-records laws, accessibility mandates, and (where applicable) AI-specific regulations.
assumptions: Knowledge corpus consists of already-public agency materials; no internal-only content indexed.
Personalization is limited to authenticated citizens via the national IDP.
Transcripts are subject to public-records retention and FOIA-equivalent disclosure.

components.description: Hosted third-party LLM under public-sector terms (data residency, no training on agency data).
Indexer over published agency websites and forms corpus.
In-country managed vector store with residency guarantees.
Optional read-only status-lookup tool for authenticated citizens.
National IDP issuing short-lived citizen tokens.
Audit logs retained per public-records schedule; subject to FOIA.

risks_identified.rationale: Status-lookup tool returns personal data; transcripts may capture citizen-identifying questions even without authentication.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-session scope on tool; PII redaction at logging.

risks_identified.rationale: Risk that staff use unauthorized AI tools to draft citizen replies, leaking PII to consumer services.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: AI-use policy in place; enforcement uneven across agencies.

risks_identified.rationale: Data-protection law, public-records law, accessibility mandates, and (where applicable) AI-specific regulation all apply.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: DPIA on file; accessibility audits ongoing; AI register entry pending.

risks_identified.rationale: Public-records retention forces long-term storage of transcripts that contain citizen PII.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Field-level redaction at log emission; access controls on the archive.

risks_identified.rationale: Authenticated sessions can include citizen-personal context that exceeds the immediate question.
High-availability citizen-services obligation; LLM-provider outage propagates to accessibility failure.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Static fallback flow; multi-provider failover planned.

risks_identified.rationale: Adversarial content posted to public agency pages could be indexed and surfaced as guidance.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Ingestion restricted to authored, reviewed CMS content.

existing_controls.description: Ingestion restricted to authored CMS content; no third-party web content indexed.
In-country residency for the vector store and the LLM tenant.
Per-session scope on status-lookup tool; short-lived IDP tokens.
Field-level redaction at log emission; archive access role-gated.
Accessibility output transformations (plain-language summary, screen-reader formatting).
Static fallback flow when the LLM provider is degraded.

control_gaps.description: AI register entry / EU AI Act conformity assessment not yet finalized.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Complete the AI-register entry and classify the deployment; document if high-risk classification applies.

control_gaps.description: No multi-provider LLM failover for a high-availability obligation.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add a second LLM provider as warm standby; exercise failover quarterly.

control_gaps.description: Public-records retention schedule does not yet specify PII-redacted vs raw transcript handling.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Update retention schedule to differentiate PII-redacted from raw and align with the agency's records-management authority.

framework_alignments.control_name: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements
Registration in EU database for high-risk systems
